summary: Iteration_48 — Opt-in PUBLIC library mode (Goodreads-style). Tested backend endpoints (account/public-library-visibility GET+PUT, /users/{u}/public-library) and frontend (Account toggle, PublicLibraryView page, PublicCoverProfile link). All critical assertions pass — including the SAME 404 for both 'fake handle' and 'exists-but-not-opted-in', and AV-infected book omission. 11/11 backend pytest cases pass. 9/9 frontend UI flows confirmed (the only 'failure' was my own aria-checked assertion — the toggle behavior was proven by both toasts appearing in correct order and backend flag flipping).
critical_code_review_comments: friend_library.py:283 — public endpoint also gates on approval_status != 'approved' (treats unapproved users as 404). Good: this also avoids leaking 'user exists but is suspended'. Note: pending users who opt-in will not be reachable; that may be intentional (only approved users get a public URL).

friend_library.py:298 — book listing relies on a non-indexed sort on title; for libraries with many books this could become slow. Consider an index on (user_id, title) or a sane default limit guard. limit clamping (1..500) is correct.

friend_library.py:286-292 — query does NOT filter by visibility/shared-status on the books themselves (only av_status!=infected). This means EVERY book the user has uploaded is public once they opt in (including books they may have marked 'private' on the per-book level if such a flag exists). Verify this is intentional vs leaking individually-hidden books.

PublicCoverProfile.jsx:79 — the lazy probe hits /users/{username}/public-library?limit=1 for EVERY profile load, even when the visitor will never click. Fine at current scale but caches/HEAD endpoint would be cheaper.

PublicLibraryView.jsx — fandom chip data-testid replaces only spaces with underscores (`f.fandom.replace(/\s+/g, '_')`). Fandoms with punctuation (e.g. 'Marvel: Civil War') would still produce a testid with ':' in it — works but escape with care in tests. Multi-word fandoms confirmed working (Harry Potter -> Harry_Potter).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (9/9 functional flows; 1 false-alarm on aria-checked attribute that the Radix Switch doesn't expose)

test_credentials: Fresh users created via POST /api/auth/register. Saved to /tmp/fe_users.json: opted-in user TEST_fe_opted_e24c9d@example.com / hunter2pw! / @febopta508d (library_visible_to_public=True, 2 clean books + 1 infected). Not-opted-in TEST_fe_priv_7eb2f8@example.com / @febpri4c63f. No-handle TEST_fe_nohandle_c3f849@example.com.
seed_data_creation: 3 fresh users (opted-in / not-opted-in / no-handle), 3 books (2 clean Harry Potter + Marvel; 1 av_status='infected') inserted directly to mongo for opted-in user. All TEST_-prefixed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration_48 feature (opt-in public library) is GREEN end-to-end. Key verified behaviors: (a) handle-enumeration prevention — fake handle and 'exists but not opted in' both return 404; (b) AV-infected books filtered out of public response; (c) q substring filter requires >=2 chars; (d) limit clamps to [1,500]; (e) toggle persists across reload; (f) no-handle users are blocked client-side with a 'Claim a @handle' toast AND backend flag stays false; (g) PublicCoverProfile 'Browse library' link only appears for opted-in users (lazy probed via the public-library endpoint). Test file lives at /app/tests/ (NOT /app/backend/tests/ — keep it outside backend dir to avoid uvicorn watchfiles reload loops).
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. One self-inflicted Playwright assertion error (Radix Switch doesn't expose aria-checked); cross-verified the toggle works via both toast text appearances + backend flag values.
